response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses 1/2 ounce every day. Over the course of a leap year, which has 366 days, she will use 1/2 ounce per day × 366 days = 183 ounces of shampoo. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, she will need to buy [number_of_bottles] bottles to cover her needs. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so she needs to calculate how many bottles are required to reach at least 183 ounces. For example, if she buys 13 bottles, that would give her 182 ounces, which is just short of her needs, so she would need to buy [14] bottles to have enough shampoo.

[address] is a placeholder for the specific number of bottles needed. The second paragraph provides the calculation based on the number of days in a leap year and the amount of shampoo used per day.
